title: Why Is Diamondback (FANG) Down 4.1% Since Last Earnings Report?

title: Keep an Eye on 3 Permian Stocks as Oil Price is Still Healthy
text: Exploration and production businesses are still favorable as crude price remains healthy. Thus, Diamondback (FANG), Pioneer (PXD) & Matador (MTDR) are poised to gain.

title: Meet the Nasdaq's 3 Biggest Dividend Payers
text: It makes sense. The tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 consists largely of cyclical companies that prefer to reinvest profits in growth projects instead of paying them out as dividends.

title: The Days of Gushing Oil-Fueled Dividends Appear to Be Over
text: Pioneer Natural Resources is refining its capital return framework. Diamondback Energy is using its flexibility to repurchase shares.

title: 43 May 2023 Ideal Dividend Large-Cap High-Yield Stocks
text: Dogsofthedow.com published this May list of 50 Large Cap High Dividend stocks 5/5/23. Dogsofthedow.com published this list for investors who “don't want to simply focus on high dividend yield only to discover the stock price plunging or the corporation has gone belly-up."

title: These Oil Stocks Think They're Incredible Bargain Buys Right Now
text: Oil companies are starting to shift their priorities from paying gushing dividends to repurchasing shares. Diamondback Energy used its flexible capital return strategy to take advantage of last quarter's volatility.

title: 2 Investment-Grade Stocks With Big Dividends: Diamondback And Simon Property
text: We look at two bargain stocks with low price-earnings (P/E) ratios and big growth prospects. Pick #1: Diamondback (FANG) is an investment-grade company and carries a forward P/E ratio of just 6 times despite high growth.

title: Diamondback (FANG) Q1 Earnings Miss Estimates on Lower Prices
text: For 2023, Diamondback Energy (FANG) forecast a capital spending budget between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion and looks to pump 430,000-440,000 BOE/d of hydrocarbon.

title: Diamondback Energy (FANG) Lags Q1 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Diamondback Energy (FANG) came out with quarterly earnings of $4.10 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $4.36 per share. This compares to earnings of $5.20 per share a year ago.
